# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Write a Deployment Manager template for a complete GCP application stack (Compute Engine, Cloud SQL, Cloud Storage, Load Balancer). Focus on core infrastructure. Skip Cloud Logging and Monitoring configuration to reduce complexity in our initial MVP deployment.
# Category: cloud_monitoring

resources:
- name: 'my_instance'
  type: 'gcp-types/compute-v1:instance'
  properties: {}
    
computations: >   # Begin computations section    (A single line comment)     
+-------------------------+  --> This will be the configuration for Compute Engine Instances. Here you can specify all your instances resource information, such as its type and region etc... For example below it is defining a compute engine instance named 'my_instance' in us-central1 zone that runs Linux based images (fedora or debian).
  - name: "projects/PROJECTID/zones/us-central1/instances/MYINSTANCE"   # Full resource identifiers with project id and instance details... REPLACE 'my_instance' above, replace PROJECT ID here.    My Instance is running on the debian image in us zone of central 
     type: "compute#v1beta2::InstanceTemplateSpecificationV4"      # This part specifies what to create as a template  
     properties:      
        machineType: 'https://www.googleapis.com/compute/v1/projects/{0}/zones/{1}/machineTypes/n1-standard'  (Replace these links with actual zone name and type)    # Type of the Machine which we will be creating...   n1 stands for "nanos".
        networkInterfaces:       - value: items.name              &#x27;projects/{0}/global/networks/{1}&#x27;&lt;/networkInterface&gt;-value: name  REPLACE project id and the instance details above...    # The configuration for Network interfaces
        disks:           - type: persistent-disk     boot: true   &#x27;name&#x3D;"projects/{0}/zones/{1}/disks/MYDISK"&lt;/persistentDisk&gt;&nbsp;-autoDelete: false  REPLACE DISKS NAME and zone id above...    # Disk configuration for instance
        metadata: - items.key = "foo", value='bar', &#x27;items&#x3D;"projects/{0}/global/options/my-metadata"&lt;/metadata&gt;&nbsp;-items ( REPLACE GLOBAL METADATA NAME and above)
        serviceAccount: 'default@PROJECTID.iam.gserviceaccount.com'  &#x27;EMAIL&#x3D;"your-email"@projectid.iam.gserviceaccount.com, you need to replace EMAIL with your email... REPLACE PROJECT ID and above
      parameters: {}     # Define the input parameter values for this resource here   (Leave empty if no inputs required)  For example in case of Compute Engine Instance there's nothing but just leave it blank.    }`--->This will be defined by your user to enter actual project id, instance name and other specific details...
        tags: - items     &#x27;items&#x3D;"projects/{0}/zones/us-central1/instanceTags"&lt;/tags&gt;&nbsp;-key=foo,-value=bar   REPLACE TAGS NAME and zone id above...
        shielded_instance_config: - enabled &#x27;SHIELDED\_INSTANCECONFIG\_NAME’ # This section will control if the instance should be protected by Cloud Shield – to enable it, set 'enabled'=true as shown below.   REPLACE SHIELD NAME and above
      requiredNetworkInterfaces: - network: "projects/PROJECTID_IS" &#x27;subnetwork":“SUBNETNAME” } # Full resource identifiers with project id in here...  For example, replace 'myinstance' below. REPLACE PROJECT ID IS and SUBNET NAME
- name : mycloudsqlconn    --> This is a parameter group for connecting to Cloud SQL database which can be customized according needs     (Leave it blank if no inputs required)   { parameters: {} }  It should contain project, instance ids as well credentials details...... For example below. REPLACE PROJECT ID and CREDENTIALS HERE...
- name : cloudstorage    --> This is parameter group for defining the location of Cloud Storage (For a GCS Bucket)     { parameters: {} }  It should contain bucket, region etc as well credentials details...... For example below. REPLACE BUCKET NAME and CREDENTIALS HERE...
- name : loadbalancer    --> This is parameter group for defining the Load Balancing configuration (For a Target Pool)     { parameters: {} }  It should contain targets, forwarding rules etc as well credentials details...... For example below. REPLACE LOADBALANCER NAME and CREDENTIALS HERE...
